text_words.notes: Appears to be a fictional or cipher script resembling stylized Middle Eastern/Aramaic-like characters. Not a real natural language. Approximately 5 lines.
Second paragraph, approximately 8 lines of the same fictional script.
Third paragraph, approximately 4 lines of the same fictional script.

symbols.description: Two circular sigil drawings side by side at the bottom of the page. Left sigil: circle divided by a cross/plus into four quadrants, each containing small circles (3-4 per quadrant), with a small mark or numeral at the top center inside the circle. Right sigil: more complex circular composition with a central column/tree motif, what appears to be small human figures (a larger standing figure and smaller figures), an architectural element resembling a gate or temple with pillars/columns, small circles in the lower corners (possibly wheels or rosettes), and dots/sand-like texture at the bottom.

drawings.subject: sigil_circle_left
drawings.description: Geometric sigil: circle with cross/plus dividing it into four quadrants, populated with small open circles. A small mark at top inside circle.

drawings.subject: sigil_circle_right
drawings.description: Figurative sigil: circle containing a central vertical element (tree/column) with a figure on top, smaller human figures on either side, a horizontal architectural band with pillars/columns across the middle, two small rosette/wheel symbols in lower left and right, and dots at the base.

mixed_media_regions.description: Combined region at bottom containing two hand-drawn sigils: the right sigil mixes text-like marks, symbolic figures (anthropomorphic), geometric elements (cross, circles, wheels) and architectural drawing (pillars/gate) — clearly a mixed-media magical/ritual diagram accompanying the script text above.

uncertain: All textual content: the script is a fictional/constructed/cipher alphabet and cannot be reliably transcribed character by character. Individual glyphs resemble stylized Semitic letter forms (resembling Hebrew/Aramaic/Thaana-like shapes) but do not correspond to a known natural language.
Whether the small mark at the top of the left sigil is a numeral, letter, or other symbol is unclear.
The exact identity/meaning of the figures in the right sigil (deity? person? stylized human?) cannot be determined.
The two small rosette/wheel-like circles in the lower corners of the right sigil are ambiguous symbols.
The small mark/numeral at the apex of the right sigil's central column is not identifiable.
